questions.question: Explain the Fundamental Theorem of Counting with an example.
keyPoints: States if task A can be done in m ways and task B in n ways, together they can be done in m×n ways
Applies to sequential independent tasks
Example: 4 shirts and 3 pants give 4×3 = 12 outfits

questions.question: What is the difference between permutations and combinations? Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Permutations: arrangements where ORDER MATTERS
Combinations: selections where ORDER DOESN'T MATTER
Example Perm: arranging 3 people in 3 seats (6 ways)
Example Comb: choosing 3 committee members from 10 people (120 ways)

questions.question: State and prove the Pigeonhole Principle.
keyPoints: If n items are put into m boxes and n > m, then some box contains more than one item
Proof by contradiction: assume each box has at most 1 item, then total ≤ m < n, contradiction
Used to prove existence of certain configurations

questions.question: Find the number of ways to form a 3-digit number using digits 1,2,3,4,5 (digits may be repeated).
keyPoints: Hundreds place: 5 choices (1-5)
Tens place: 5 choices (repetition allowed)
Ones place: 5 choices
Total: 5 × 5 × 5 = 125 numbers

questions.question: Explain the Inclusion-Exclusion Principle for counting elements in the union of two sets.
keyPoints: Add individual set sizes, subtract intersection counted twice
Ensures elements in both sets are not double-counted

questions.question: In how many ways can 7 friends be seated around a circular table?
keyPoints: For circular arrangements, fix one person's position
Remaining 6 friends can be arranged in 6! = 720 ways
General formula: (n-1)! for n distinct objects in a circle

questions.question: How many words can be formed from the letters of 'ENGINEERING'? How many of these (a) begin with E, (b) have all vowels together?
keyPoints: Letters: E(3), N(3), G(2), I(2), R(1) — total 11 letters
(a) Begin with E: Fix E at start, arrange remaining 10 letters: 10!/(2!3!2!2!) = 151200
(b) Treat EEE as one unit, also group vowels (E,E,E,I,I) together — complex calculation required

questions.question: Prove that C(n,r) = C(n,n-r). Also prove Pascal's identity: C(n,r) = C(n-1,r-1) + C(n-1,r).
Find the number of ways to distribute 8 identical balls into 4 distinct boxes (a) if boxes can be empty (b) if no box can be empty.
keyPoints: (a) Using stars and bars: C(8+4-1, 4-1) = C(11,3) = 165 ways
(b) First place 1 ball in each box: use 4 balls, remaining 4 to distribute freely

questions.question: Find the number of ways to select 5 cards from a standard deck of 52 cards containing (a) exactly 2 hearts (b) at least 2 hearts.
keyPoints: Total cards: 52, Hearts: 13, Non-hearts: 39
(a) Choose 2 hearts from 13: C(13,2), choose 3 non-hearts from 39: C(39,3). Total = C(13,2)×C(39,3) = 78×9139 = 712842
(b) At least 2 hearts: sum of C(13,r)×C(39,5-r) for r=2,3,4,5 plus r can be 0,1 too large to subtract from total
Easier: Total C(52,5) - [no hearts: C(39,5)] - [1 heart: C(13,1)×C(39,4)]

questions.question: Using Binomial Theorem, find the coefficient of x^5 in (x+2)^8 and also find the sum of coefficients in (3x-2)^7.
keyPoints: (x+2)^8: General term T(r+1) = C(8,r) x^(8-r) 2^r. For x^5, 8-r=5 so r=3
(3x-2)^7: Sum of coefficients = (3-2)^7 = 1^7 = 1 (substitute x=1)
